translated: ProSeka AfterTalk: Colors of Pure Sense Edition
Hello, everyone. Good evening.
I'm Suzuki Minori, the voice of Ena Shinonome from Nightcord at 25:00.
In this ProSeka AfterTalk, we'll look back on the event while reading the messages you've sent in.
Today's episode covers the Colors of Pure Sense event.
I hope you all enjoyed the story!
Thank you so much.
Between the Thanksgiving event and the stage greetings for the movie,
things have been quite busy with real-life cast events lately.
And then this Niigo event, Ena's story event, started right away.
I'm sure it's been a whirlwind for everyone, but I hope you've been enjoying it all.
We previously asked for messages for this AfterTalk via the official ProSeka Discord and email.
Thank you all for writing in.
I honestly wish the staff would let me sneak a peek at every single one.
I'm really curious to hear what you all thought.
I'll be sharing some of those messages during the show,
so please look forward to that.
Also, please post your thoughts on the event or this stream using the hashtag #ProSekaAfterTalk.
I'm also keeping an eye on the YouTube chat,
so I'll be picking out comments that catch my eye.
All right.
Let's see...
I see comments like "It was a great story."
And people saying "Kon-Niigo!"
Kon-Niigo to you all!
Also, we have the AfterLive starting at 10:00 PM,
and the 3DMV for this event will be released at 10:05 PM.
I'd love to watch those together with everyone.
I want to make sure we stay on schedule, but I also want to spend a good amount of time on the story video.
I was a bit selfish and asked to make the story segment longer than usual,
so I hope you'll stick around and watch it with me.
Now, let's start talking about the story
for Colors of Pure Sense.
What did you all think of this story?
Let's look back on it together while I read your messages.
This is actually my first time doing the renewed version of ProSeka AfterTalk.
It's been about a year, and back then it was the old format.
So, I'm really happy to be weaving this show together using your words.
This first message is from Amelia Time.
"This is my first time sending in my thoughts."
Thank you so much!
"The scene that left the biggest impression on me was when Ena deleted her selfie account."
"I felt her growth when she, who used to run away from her art, finally decided to face it head-on."
"Like Ena, I was also afraid to face myself and kept running away,"
"but seeing Ena's positive attitude gave me a push, and I want to try my best too."
"Suzuki-san, what kind of feelings"
"did you put into your performance of Ena this time?"
Thank you for the wonderful message.
Well...
Amelia Time, that was a lovely letter. Let's see...
I'll talk more about this while we watch the story later, but regarding Ena this time,
and also when singing recent Niigo songs, there's something I've been conscious of.
As her voice actress, I personally tell myself, "I won't let her go back to the old Ena."
Even if it's slow, Ena is moving forward bit by bit. Because of the nature of Niigo's music, the lyrics can often be quite pessimistic,
and the songs themselves have a certain heavy atmosphere.
But I try not to let her get pulled too far into that. Honestly, as a song or a performance,
adding a hint of tears or a negative nuance might make it more striking to the listener,
or make people think, "Oh, I love the way she delivered that phrase."
There are definitely parts that catch your ear because of that.
But I realized that's just my own desire for validation as an actress.
I want to make sure I put the fact that Ena is looking forward, even if just a little, into her songs and lines.
So even with the same words, I try to deliver them so you can feel her progress.
I've been gradually increasing that sense of positivity based on the story.
That's what I've been keeping in mind
while playing her this time.
And...
Yeah.
In this event and in recent songs, she's not as cynical about things anymore.
I think Ena now truly understands that the world is warmer than she thought,
and that the people around her are there to help her.
I made sure to reflect that in my performance.
Now, moving on,
I'll read one more message.
This one is from Sudachi Udon.
"Minori-san, good evening."
Yes, good evening!
"I've finished reading Colors of Pure Sense."
Thank you very much.
"The scene that stuck with me was in Episode 7, where Ena gets so excited because she's having so much fun drawing."
"Ena, who could only boost her self-esteem through others' praise on her selfie account, finally realized she's fine just as she is."
